name: Rudy Collins
abstract: Rudy Collins (July 24, 1934 – August 15, 1988) was an American jazz drummer born in New York City. Collins played trombone in high school and started on drums at that time as well.

concerts.label: An Evening of Song with Cleo Laine and John Dankworth

works.t: The Mikado: The Sun the Moon and I
On a Clear Day You Can See Forever: On a Clear Day (You Can See Forever)
I'm Gonna Sit Right Down and Write Myself a Letter

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I used that rhythm section: Kenny, Chris and Rudy Collins. ... We recorded an album called “You Had Better Listen” in 1967.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: Rudy Collins [phonetic sp.] the drummer... And with George Stefano, Rudy Collins, Mike Longwell, and myself. And Dizzy.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: He would often tell Rudy Collins what he wanted... my last year there, both Chris White and Rudy Collins left.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
